name: Kolach (Braided Bread)
recipeIngredient: 1 large Egg, Beaten With 1 Teaspoon Water, For Glaze
recipeInstructions:
1. Dissolve 1 teaspoon sugar in the water and sprinkle the yeast over it. Add 3 teaspoons sugar, stir gently, and let stand for 10 minutes.
2. Combine the yeast with the milk (lightly beaten), eggs, salt, oil, and flour. Beat until smooth. Cover and let rise in a warm place until light and bubbly, about 1 hour.
3. Mix in enough of the remaining flour to make a light, slightly sticky dough, then knead in the bowl until smooth and elastic. The heavier the dough, the heavier the bread.
4. Form into a ball and place in a lightly oiled bowl, turning once to oil the top. Cover with a linen dish towel soaked in hot water and then wrung out until damp only. Let rise in a warm place until double in bulk.
5. Punch down and allow to rise again, slightly. Divide the dough into 3 parts. Divide one part into thirds, rolling each into a rope 8 to 10 inches long and 1 1/2 inches thick.
6. Braid together, starting at the middle and turning the ends under. Repeat with each of the remaining parts of the dough. Place the loaves on buttered baking sheets. Do not crowd, as the loaves will double in size.
7. Cover with the damp towel and let rise until almost double. Brush with the egg glaze and sprinkle with poppy seeds.
8. Bake in a preheated 375 Degrees F oven for at least 1 hour, or until golden. Test by rapping the bottom; there should be a nice hollow sound. Cool on wire racks until cold before cutting.
